entry.title: Employee Time Off Request auto approval before Payroll cutoff date
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a requirement to auto approve the vacation leave request if it is not approved by manager before the payroll cut off date.

Consider - Payroll Run Date - 28-Feb (Every 14 days)

Scenario 1:

Employee applied leave on 10-Feb as below:

Employee Time Off Start Date - 15-Feb

Employee Time Off End Date - 20-Feb

If manager doesn't approve by 27-Feb then it should be automatically approved.

Scenario 2:

Employee applied leave on 10-Feb as below:

Employee Time Off Start Date - 15-Feb

Employee Time Off End Date - 1-Mar

If manager doesn't approve by 13-Mar (as next payroll run date is 14-Mar) then it should be automatically approved.

understand there is no standard approach for this requirement any other means (using CPI, Integration Center) can this be achieved.

 

Thanks,

Sriram

entry.title: How to Report Tax on Unreturned Deposits with SAF-T for Poland: Navigating JPK_V7K/M(3) Requirements
entry.content:
In the evolving landscape of taxation and compliance, staying updated with the latest legal requirements is crucial for businesses operating in Poland.  

Following our previous blog post, “Understanding the New Tags in the SAF-T Poland VAT Report (JPK_V7M(3) and JPK_V7K(3))” this article delves into how to specifically handle tax on unreturned deposits within the JPK_V7K(3) and JPK_V7M(3). 


Introduction to the New Reporting Requirements 

The Polish Ministry of Finance has introduced new requirements for the SAF-T Poland VAT Report to address the tax processing of unreturned deposits for businesses dealing in products with refundable beverage packaging.  

Businesses need to report the tax amount on non-returned deposits collected for products in beverage packaging, covered by the deposit system. This tax is payable by representative entities as outlined in Article 17b of the Act.  

A new element – K_360 – was introduced in the report structure for output tax in JPK_V7M and JPK_V7K for this purpose.  

If a business is not involved in such transactions, the corresponding field remains blank. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Implementing the New Regulation 

Create New VAT Output Tax Codes 

Create new VAT output tax codes specific to non-refundable deposits, like in the following example: 

AA 

Output tax - on the non-refundable deposit 5% 

AB 

Output tax - on the non-refundable deposit  8% 

AC 

Output tax - on the non-refundable deposit 23% 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Configure Your System 

You need to map the newly defined tax codes to the appropriate K* fields, specifically K_15 to K_20, in Map Tax Codes to SAF-T Elements.  

 

 

The tax codes also need to be defined in Define Special Mappings for SAF-T Elements with the following values: 

  • Additional Classification: 03 
  • VAT Document Type: WEW 

 

 

Document Reference 

As a reference in the posted document, enter the text “SYSTEM KAUCYJNY” together with the name and number of the company of the representative entity that is responsible for the deposit payment to the bank account.  

Why This Matters 

Using these tags helps you to categorize accurately your transaction, ensuring compliance with Polish tax regulations, while reducing reporting errors.  

For more detailed information about the changes in the JPK_V7M(3) and JPK_V7K(3) structures, see the Regulatory Change Manager.   

Understanding and implementing these changes is crucial for businesses involved with products featuring deposit-return systems. By aligning your reporting processes with the new SAF-T Poland VAT requirements, you ensure compliance, mitigate errors, and enhance the efficiency of your tax operations.

entry.title: How to influence ServiceNOW incident priority when generating incidents from Cloud ALM
entry.content:
We have configured ServiceNOW integration in Cloud ALM and we are able to generate incidents successfully, but there is nothing in the documentation on how to setup different INC priorities. For some alerts we require a HIGH priority to be generated, but now all INC are generated as Medium.




------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to influence ServiceNOW incident priority when ge...
entry.content:
The priority of the Service Now Incident is defined in the mapping structure of the integration, so in case its required to adjust the default priority and/or create rules to open cases with different priorities, please go to the Service Now mapping and make the requirement adjustments.

Example, if the Built-In mapping(offered by SAP) is in use the following structure(or very similar) should be in the mapping:
         "structure": {
                "request": {
                    "location":"#{serviceId}",
                    "correlation_id":"#{id}",
                    "work_notes":"#{workNotes}",
                    "contact_type":"Self-service",
                    "category":"Inquiry",
                    "subcategory":"Internal Application",
                    "severity":"2",
                    "business_impact":"Issue reported by SAP Cloud ALM",
                    "impact":"${SWITCH(switchImpact,rating)}",
                    "short_description":"[Cloud ALM for Operations] #{subject}",
                    "description":"Service Name: #{tags.serviceName} \nService Type: #{tags.serviceTypeLabel}",
                    "comments":"#{htmlBody}"

Details:
- The 'severity' is hardcoded, so all tickets in service now will be created with severity 2, in this case it is possible simply change it to 1,3,4, so all tickets will be created with this defined severity. Or you can create a rule to change the severity based on certain event/alert information.
- The 'impact' is already based on the alert rating, so this may be use as reference.

The event fields used in the API integration can be checked here:
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/apis/event-situation
More details on the mapping configuration can be verified here:
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/apis/external-api-management-mapping

entry.title: Re: How to grant or revoke privileges/permissions to spec...
entry.content:
Hello,

Yes, it is possible to revoke specific permissions for PUBLIC or for roles/users.

  • Document explaining about how the grant/revoke commands works for ASE and how it impacts the object: Granting and Revoking Object Permissions to a Group | SAP Help Portal
  • Document about the revokes permissions from users, roles, or groups: revoke | SAP Help Portal
  • Document to grant or revoke permissions to a user: Granting and Revoking Object Permissions to a User | SAP Help Portal

Some examples are provided in this document for the PUBLIC group: 

  1. Revoke select permission for specific table:

    revoke select on get_appcontext from public

  2. Revoke update permission for specific table:

    revoke update
    on titles (price, advance)
    from public
 

best regards.

entry.title: Re: PR/PO costing duplicated to CC and WBS
entry.content:
Hi,
On this subject please refer to the attached KBA 1916523 and the FAQ note note 496082 24th Question:
What is the correct procedure for changing account assignment data?

In order to clear previous accounting data the account assignment category has to be completely cleared
and then reset as new, otherwise the system assumes the previous entries to be manual and does not overwrite them. 

Thanks and regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Migrated implicit enhancement on object not enhanceable anymore
entry.content:
I'm upgrading from ECC 6.0 EHP 7 to S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 ON-PREMISE, and I've got one implicit enhancement at the beginning of REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY (function group SLVC_FULLSCREEN), but it shows an adjustment issue:

If I press the Change button to adjust it, the exception class CX_ENH_SEEF_BASE_NOT_ENHAN is raised (NB: there's no error message, it's directly the exception text):

Object FUGR SLVC_FULLSCREEN cannot be enhanced.
The package cannot be enhanced.

It's also impossible to modify the enhancement code.

Although it says it cannot be enhanced, the enhancement code is active and I can debug it.

What can I do to keep and adjust this enhancement?

Thanks.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Migrated implicit enhancement on object not enhanceable anymore
entry.content:
Unfortunately, for these enhancements to objects belonging to packages which cannot be enhanced, you may only delete this enhancement as it's non-sense to keep it if it cannot be maintained, and there are two solutions:

  1. Either do a modification of the standard if the ABAP system is ON-PREMISE
  2. Or find a solution suitable to your exact custom situation (for instance, enhance the code which calls REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY if the enhancement was limited to very specific calls).

entry.title: SE20 Composite Enh Impl: Activation/Generation tool for ZEN_XXXX not found (message SWB_TOOL037)
entry.content:
In my ABAP 7.58 SP 1 system, I go to the transaction SE20, I enter in change mode of an existing Composite Enhancement Implementation, I change the description and I activate it. It's activating, but it also displays a warning message:

Activation/Generation tool for ZEN_XXXX not found (message SWB_TOOL037)

For information, this message is sent by the subroutine GENERATE_ACTIVE_OBJECT of SAPLSEWORKINGAREA:

What does this message mean? As the object is activated, is it okay if I ignore this message?

Thanks.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: SE20 Composite Enh Impl: Activation/Generation tool for ZEN_XXXX not found (message SWB_TOOL037)
entry.content:
There's no SAP note about this message, the object is activated, so yes you may ignore this message. If in doubt, contact the SAP Support.

For information, "XF" represents the internal code of the ABAP Workbench tool for composite enhancement implementations. XF is registered in the table EUOBJEDIT, but not in the table WBOBJECTTYPES, I guess it's the reason why this message is sent, but I have no idea why it's considered important to display such a warning.

entry.title: Re: International Editable SALV Day 2026 – Year 18
entry.content:
Hi @Maxx2 ,

First of all, thanks for reaching out. I appreciate it.
After all we are a community here and it is by sharing ideas and experiences that we can all grow 🙂 

I have been involved with the on-stack HR module for about two years now and feel your pain, or perhaps I should say: exhaustion of searching.
Sadly there is indeed nothing in terms of CDS for the HR module out of the box.
The strategic decision would be to move to SuccessFactors for HR, this is where SAP still invests in the HR matters.
Reality shows that not everything that was set up in the classic HR module exists in SuccesFactors (I should say Yet, due to the ongoing improvements to it)  and on top of that the classic module allowed for a lot of extension options to tailor it to individual needs, which might be more limited in SuccesFactors. I have no hands on experience with SuccesFactors, but believe it works with a kind of extensibility framework like what is the only option in the pure SAP cloud ERP.

Now to switch over to the topic of the classic HR tables (IT* and P*) and CDS.
We tackled it by building our own model on top of those tables from the ground up, using the virtual data model rules.
Thus, base CDS views on top of tables, Interface views on top of that in different layers to make all connections (via associations) that were needed, and then finally consumption views when creating  RAP applications on top of the model.
I cannot share the model because I have signed non-disclosure agreements that state that developments made for the customer may not go out. Also, it might not fit everyone anyway due to the high configurability on how the full HR is set up.
My advise is to start building out a model either focus on it at full or build it on a per need basis, just keep reusability in mind. The whole idea to put time in it is also: if SAP would ever decide to bring the HR module back on-stack and continue with it (I don't think it is currently on the roadmaps, but you never know) the standard things that might come can be plugged in under the top views of the custom model. Sure some adaptation might be required but it makes us as ready as can be ( we can always dream, right 😊 ).

Then for the RAP part,
For display reports it can simply be build on top of the custom HR model using fiori elements. It can be done without RAP by simply using the odata publish annotation, but by using RAP (managed) it is possible to use oData V4 and allows for additions later on if required.
For applications that do some actual processing it would be Managed RAP with unmanaged save or fully unmanaged RAP depending on the requirements.
EML cannot be used for these RAP, and that can bring some additional overhead. Instead we have to call classic functions to manipulate the HR information if that is required. This means it is not completely clean core, it is as cloud ready as we can get it at current time. It can also be considered to put wrapper functions around the standard function, but personally I don't see the added value there.
Not sure how experienced you are with RAP, if you need training on that SAP has some learning journeys for it.
Given they tend to be a bit on the easy side to reflect realistic cases, it is a starting point
https://learning.sap.com/courses/learn-the-basics-of-abap-programming-on-sap-btp/building-an-abap-restful-application 

Hopefully I could clarify some of you questions or doubts and could help you on diciding on a strategy to move forward.

Kr,
Joery

entry.title: Re: check is not getting assigned in public cloud
entry.content:
Dear @muhammad_yousufali,

In Public cloud you typically won’t get an automatically determined check number from Post Outgoing Payments (F1612). SAP explicitly points out that check output configuration is not applicable to Post Outgoing Payments and for an automatically generated check number / printable check you should use Create Single Payment (F0743) or Manage Automatic Payments (F0770)

SAP Notes below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2844125

What to do instead:

Use Create Single Payment (F0743) and pay with payment method C (Check) the system will generate the check number automatically and you can manage/print via outgoing checks. Look at SAP Note 2844125 above

IMPORTANT: In Public Cloud most often checkbook/check lot setup or check lot exhaustion where Manage Automatic Payments does NOT assign a check number. SAP troubleshooting points are:

Point 1. Fix the check lot (Checkbook) setup in Manage Checkbooks (F1577)

For check payments via the payment run SAP requires:

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2706870

  • Only one checkbook chain per bank account
  • All checkbooks in the chain are:
    • Sequential = Yes
    • Payment method = C
  • If you create a new checkbook, it must be maintained as Successor in the chain otherwise checks won’t be created

Point 2. Make sure you didn’t run out of check numbers

If the run creates more payments than remaining checks the system can’t determine a check lot for some payments and the check printing job can cancel. Maintain sufficient ranges and successor checkbooks

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3605460

Point 3. Read the payment run log message

Look specifically for messages like:

  • No check lot could be determined
  • Errors about multiple lots permitted / chain inconsistency

SAP Notes below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3605460

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2706870

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Any standard utility in ABAP to retrieve a version of abap dev object from Remote system
entry.content:
"API" is usually a word for interfaces released to be used by customers, but the function modules for working with versions and transport requests are not released by SAP so you may use them at your own risk.

What you could do:

  1. I think it's best to create an RFC-enabled Z function module to be installed on your PROD system, which calls:
    1. TRINT_INSERT_COMM to create a ToC,
    2. TRINT_RELEASE_REQUEST to release the ToC. There's BAPI_CTREQUEST_RELEASE too, but sometimes it's a little bit limited.
    3. Read the created files in the cofiles and data directories (be careful the release may run asynchronously, so you may need to wait a little bit), return the data from these files.
  2. In the DEV system:
    1. Create these files in the cofiles and data directories of your DEV system (if the transport domain of DEV and PRD are different),
    2. Add the transport request to the import queue via TMS_MGR_GREP_TRANSPORT_QUEUE and TMS_MGR_FORWARD_TR_REQUEST
    3. Import via TMS_MGR_IMPORT_TR_REQUEST.
    4. NB: you may get the transport domain via TMS_CFG_GET_DOMAIN_NAME.

NB: I never saw a customer asking for such a risky program (automating things between PROD and DEV systems -- they should never be connected for security reason --, overwriting objects without control) and I would never suggest doing this, so it's really at your own risk.

Make sure that the client understands the risks.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, February 12th, 2026
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

Your 2026 Roadmap to Getting Started with ABAP AI and ABAP-1

  • ABAP AI and ABAP-1 Roadmap https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/your-2026-roadmap-to-getting-started-with-abap-ai-and-abap-1/ba-p/14312060

CAP January 2026 release

  • Release notes https://pages.github.tools.sap/cap/docs/releases/2026/jan26
  • Blog post "Modifying queries, replacing the WHERE clause" https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2026/02/04/modifying-queries-replacing-the-where-clause/
  • Hands-on SAP Dev mini-series on CXL in CAP https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2025/12/09/a-new-hands-on-sap-dev-mini-series-on-the-core-expression-language-in-cds/

Brazil CodeJam roadshow

  • SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2026 - Brazil edition 🇧🇷 - https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-roadshow-2026-brazil-edition-kicking-off-in-march-and/ba-p/14317926

CAP Customer Roundtable

  • Agenda and details https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-cloud-application-programming-model-cap-customer-roundtable-february/ba-p/14317448

Did You Know? #3 – Debugging apps on CF

  • Debugging with cds debug https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/tools/cds-cli#cds-debug

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 ABAP AI and ABAP-1 Roadmap

1:25 CAP January 2026 release

5:48 Brazil CodeJam roadshow

6:51 CAP Customer Roundtable 18 Feb

7:29 Did You Know? #3 – Debugging apps on CF

Transcription

00;00;00;10 - 00;00;05;16
Announcer Guy
This is the SAP Developer News for February 12th, 2026.

00;00;05;19 - 00;00;09;22
Mamikee Kanneh
Hi everyone. A new ABAP AI roadmap blog post just dropped

00;00;09;29 - 00;00;32;01
Mamikee Kanneh
and the message is clear. The era of wait and see for AI in ABAP development is officially over, with Joule for Developers now generally available and Agentic AI frameworks planned for 2026. The question isn't if you should use AI, it's where to start. And according to the blog post, for 90% of developers, the best starting point is Joule

00;00;32;02 - 00;00;59;28
Mamikee Kanneh
for developers inside the ABAP development tools in Eclipse. Its trained on over 250 million lines of SAP code, understand our ABAP Cloud context, and supports real developer workflows like code generation, explaining legacy code and unit test creations directly inside your IDE. And here's a big highlight. SAP has extended the free promotional period for Joule for developers until September 2026.

00;01;00;02 - 00;01;25;00
Mamikee Kanneh
The blog post also outlined three clear path forward standard development with Joule, innovation using the ABAP AI SDK and BTP free tier, and modernization with VS Code coming in 2026. Now, if you're wondering where to begin with ABAP AI this year, this roadmap is a must read. Check out the blog post in the link below to learn more.

00;01;25;02 - 00;01;58;13
DJ Adams
The January 2026 release of CAP is out, bringing with it plenty of additions and changes. This releases sees the Node.js runtime and the development kit go to 9.7 to Java Runtime to 4.7. The CDS compiler to 6.7, and the MTX libraries to 3.7. So here's a quick overview of just some of the items in the release notes. First, let's talk about Capphire, CAP’s official and already great documentation.

00;01;58;15 - 00;02;24;16
DJ Adams
So the team have made some fab renovations. This release, for example, the Getting Started topics have been improved. There are new develop and deploy topics, and a lot of the existing guides have been updated and in fact new guides have been added as well. In particular, the newly combined Security and Data Privacy Guide is definitely worth a read.

00;02;24;19 - 00;03;01;21
DJ Adams
Those of you lovely people who are joining and participating in our current Hands-on SAP Dev live stream series or watching the replays, looking under the hood at expressions in CDS will know that there's also a new reference section for the CDS expression language. CXL. Okay, moving to the NodeJS runtime context. Express five is now supported. In addition, unless we have a specific dependency on express, now we can emit it from the list in our package.json file.

00;03;01;24 - 00;03;30;09
DJ Adams
There's a new configuration option that lets us manage source folders for initial and sample or test data across different profiles, which is really nice. There's also some additions to the documentation for the clone method in the cds.ql API. This method helps us manage our query objects safely with a given clone modify forward pass in. You can see this clone method in action.

00;03;30;09 - 00;03;55;28
DJ Adams
In a recent developer, advocates post modifying queries replacing the Where clause. Moving on to the Java topic now. SAP machine SAP machine 17 will reach End of life this coming September and from April this year. Already the SAP Java Build pack will default to Java 21. So you've got some planning to do with respect to Java versions here.

00;03;56;00 - 00;04;02;05
DJ Adams
Ideally and preferably you should migrate directly to 25 if you can.

00;04;02;07 - 00;04;38;24
DJ Adams
There's now support for OData create requests that don't include a key filter in the path info part of the URL, as long as an appropriate foreign key value is provided in the payload of the request. Now moving on to the CDS editor facilities. There are some really cool new features and improvements such as multi cursor support for selection ranges, improvements to folding ranges, a reorganization of user settings and in beta faster reference search and workspace symbols.

00;04;38;26 - 00;05;12;12
DJ Adams
By the way, we can these days think of the CDS editor name as a reference to not only support in VS code via the CDS language support extension, but also in IntelliJ. There are also some improvements for Kyma, including an update for the CDS add multi-tenancy command and new CDS debug support for Kubernetes, which works pretty much just like the support for Cloud Foundry and beyond the major Capphire renovations this month, and improvements as well.

00;05;12;17 - 00;05;37;21
DJ Adams
I've already mentioned those, but there are also some smaller but just as important updates to Capphire more generally. Also summarized in this month's release notes. There's one in particular I'd like to draw our attention to, which is the fact that we now need to use the Node.js facet or --nodejs, if we want to generate a full Node.js project with package.json.

00;05;37;23 - 00;05;48;13
DJ Adams
Anyway, check out this and lots more in the documentation as well as the release notes link in the description.

00;05;48;15 - 00;06;14;11
Antonio Maradiaga
Hola SAP Developers. Big news for our Brazilian community SAP CodeJam Roadshow Estamos de volta ao Brasil. The SAP CodeJam Roadshow is back in Brazil for a special 2026 edition, and we're super excited about it. Starting the first week of March, we'll be hitting multiple cities across Brazil, running CodeJams as part of SAP Inside Track events organized by local community members.

00;06;14;13 - 00;06;50;06
Antonio Maradiaga
Then we keep the CodeJams going in April, plus a couple of meetups along the way. Topics range from AI services with CAP and generative AI hub to ABAP and Joule for developers. Spots are limited, so register early if you're in Brazil or know someone there. This is your chance for free, in-person hands-on SAP learning with other developers. Check out the full schedule and register at the blog post that I'm included in the description Até logo.

00;06;50;08 - 00;07;19;17
DJ Adams
Hello before the evening sets in here on the Macclesfield Canal. I've just got time to remind you all about the first CAP Customer Roundtable of 2026. It's next week, Wednesday, the 18th of February at 1700 hours CDT. Usual applies if you've got questions, feedback, want discuss topics, use cases with your fellow CAP developers and the CAP developer team.

00;07;19;19 - 00;07;31;03
DJ Adams
Then this is the event for you. It's free to join. You just have to register and I'll see you there.

00;07;31;06 - 00;07;55;21
DJ Adams
Did you know that it's easy to debug your CAP Node.js apps running on Cloud Foundry? Here's a sample CAP app with a simple books entity handler With CDS debug, we can start debugging locally and the debug target appears just like that. This app is also deployed to Cloud Foundry on BTP, and we can see also that SSH is enabled.

00;07;55;24 - 00;08;20;13
DJ Adams
We can use the CDS debug mechanism, but this time specify the app name. It will thereupon connect via an SSH tunnel to the app in the cloud. I'm also using the Node dev tools option because I've already got my dev tools open. And just like that, I have a debug target via that SSH tunnel and I'm connected directly to my app running on Cloud Foundry.

00;08;20;16 - 00;08;32;13
DJ Adams
Let's explore. We can set a breakpoint requesting books entity, inspect and modify data and everything else that you'd expect to do while debugging. Excellent.

entry.title: Re: Clarification Needed on SAP User Locking and Deactiva...
entry.content:
Dear @sandeep_shankarampet,

Below is how Public Cloud typically handles locking / deactivation and where it actually depends on your identity setup IAS/IPS or a corporate IdP

Question 1.

There is no single fixed SAP default number of inactivity days after which users are automatically locked. Instead S/4HANA Cloud provides an application job scheduled via the Schedule Locks for Unused Business Users / application job template Lock Unused Business Users which locks users based on a parameter you set Number Days Since Last Logon

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3589058

So the answer is:

  • Only if you scheduled/configured that job users get auto-locked after your configured number of days
  • If you didn’t schedule it S/4HANA Cloud won’t automatically lock users just because they didn’t log in

Also authentication happens at the identity provider in Public Cloud, so inactivity blocking can also be enforced in the IdP layer:

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/administrating-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/understanding-identity-management

  • In SAP Cloud Identity Authentication (IAS) there is a security feature called Block or Delete Users Due to Inactivity configured with things like Block After Day(s)

SAP Note and SAP Help below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/authentication-84f28fbd02ab484688c9a5e58be2eb5a

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003484831

  • If a user becomes Inactive in IAS then an IPS provisioning read job can propagate that state and lock the corresponding business user in S/4HANA Cloud

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3556607

Question 2.

Locking a business user is usually just an access stop. It does not automatically delete the user

For an actual deactivation/removal lifecycle SAP describes that:

  • unused business users can be automatically locked and
  • retention and ultimate deletion of blocked business users is handled via ILM (Information Lifecycle Management) policies not as an automatic immediate follow-up step

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/administrating-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/understanding-identity-management

Also in practice customers handle leavers/deactivation by options like:

  • lock the user
  • delete the user
  • change validity dates
  • remove roles

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002821839

SAP doesn’t enforce a single approval model. SAP provides the technical mechanisms the governance/approval is typically outside S/4

Question 3.

This depends heavily on where the action happens:

  • If users are being locked by the S/4HANA Lock Unused Business Users job, SAP standard behavior is simply that they become locked based on the job criteria
  • If you enforce inactivity in IAS / corporate IdP, notification behavior depends on that IdP’s configuration and features e-mail templates/policies not on S/4

Practical recommendation if you need advance notice, implement it via:

  • IdP / Identity Governance like automated emails or
  • a periodic report + workflow/email process owned by IT

If you unlock a user but the scheduled lock job is still active the user can get locked again in the next run unless they actually log in before that next execution

SAP Note and SAP Help below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3495324

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/a817aef3b51d4b0fbc4907e7adcfacd7.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Using Podman for Abap Platform Trial container on Ubuntu Server and on amd64 MacBook

Hi,

the older version of the image was removed nearly two years ago. The newer version is - or I should say, has been - available here, note the name change also:

https://hub.docker.com/r/sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial

So I adjusted the blog to reflect this location change (though I am not going to adjust all the command-line changes needed, that would be an exercise for the reader... blogs are by their nature temporary snapshots, and are not like SAP Help pages or official documentation that needs to be continually updated). However: when I just now went to check that newer version by clicking the the link provided, that has worked for two years, unfortunately today I get a 404 Not Found message.

Similarly, if you are using Docker Desktop Search function, the sapse/cloud-developer-trial image is not found. Which suggests that SAP have either temporarily or permanently removed the image from Docker Hub.

You would need to ask SAP about why that link doesn't work, I am not myself an SAP employee so have no knowledge as to whether the image will reappear or whether a new image in a new location would be published and if so when that would be.

Maybe @julieplummer20 has some knowledge about this?

BTW if at some point some image does become available again, then for MacBook users on arm64 architecture, the main ways to get the container up and running are shown in one other blog I wrote, feel free to check it out if and when the needed image is available:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/m-series-apple-chip-macbooks-and-abap-trial-containers-using-docker-and/ba-p/13593215

entry.title: External execution tools causing script and integration issues in sap environments?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m running into a confusing situation while testing some external execution tools alongside an sap environment. I was experimenting with a setup similar to what people describe when talking about delta exploits, where scripts or automation layers are injected or executed outside the core sap system.

What I’m seeing is that when these kinds of tools are used, certain sap web services, api calls, or UI-based integrations either fail to load correctly, return inconsistent data, or suddenly get blocked by browser or network security rules. In some cases, sessions are dropped, and in others, the sap front end starts behaving differently than expected.

I’m trying to figure out whether this is happening because of how these external executors interact with authentication, cookies, and request headers, or if sap’s built-in security and content-security policies are intentionally stopping these requests. I’m also wondering how this impacts sap integrations that rely on JavaScript execution, browser automation, or third-party connectors.

Has anyone here dealt with sap systems being affected by external execution or injection-style tools like this? I’d really like to understand what causes these conflicts and what the safest way is to test or isolate them without breaking sap workflows.

Thanks in advance for any insight.

entry.title: Migrating Content-Based Routing Scenarios Using the Migration Tool in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
This blog post is part of the series Master Blog: SAP Process Orchestration to SAP Integration Suite Migration – Tool and Guidance exploring how to migrate various integration patterns from SAP Process Orchestration to SAP Integration Suite using the Migration Tool. In this blog, we will focus on the Content-Based Routing (CBR) Asynchronous pattern

Objective of this Blog

The goal of this blog is to explain how the Migration Tool supports Content-Based Routing (CBR) asynchronous integration scenarios. The blog will cover the following key areas:

  • What is Content-Based Routing (CBR) in SAP Process Orchestration
  • Standard Receiver Determinations – Understanding Rules
  • No Receiver Is Found – options such as Error Message, Ignore, or Select a Default Receiver
  • How the Migration Tool migrates CBR scenarios from SAP Process Orchestration to SAP Cloud Integration

What is Content-Based Routing (CBR) in SAP Process Orchestration

Content-Based Routing (CBR) in SAP Process Orchestration is a mechanism that routes messages to the correct receiver based on the content of the message. Instead of sending a message to a fixed receiver, CBR uses Receiver Determination with Rules to evaluate message payloads (XML fields, attributes, or headers) at runtime and forward the message to only one receiver whose condition is satisfied. This enables intelligent routing without requiring any changes to the sender system.

At configuration time in SAP PO, you can define conditions that depend on the content of the message. These conditions can be applied both to receiver communication component determination and inbound interface determination. However, in this blog, we will focus specifically on receiver communication component determination, where multiple potential receivers can be configured but only one receiver is selected at runtime based on the defined routing rules.

Standard Receiver Determinations

Here you manually specify the receiver as well as conditions for forwarding the message to the specific receiver.

You can also define rules for forwarding the message to the receivers.

The following options are available:

  • Specify “Local” Rules: You define the rules specially for a specific receiver determination. This then also applies for messages where the address field in the header matches the key of the receiver determination.
  • Insert (Reusable) Rules: You use separately defined rules that can be reused in multiple receiver determinations. A rule such as this is defined as a standalone object in the Integration Directory and identified as a receiver rule.

Note: A receiver rule applies to all messages whose address header matches the object keys of the receiver determinations in which the receiver rule is used. SAP recommends using receiver rules if you want to use the same rule in different scenarios.

For more details, see Defining Standard Receiver Determinations

Receiver interfaces and Communication Channels are assigned to specific receivers: In the following example, Receiver interfaces and mappings are bound to their corresponding receivers.

NoReceiverBehavior

Specifies what happens if no receiver can be determined at runtime.

Possible values:

  • Error Message: Message processing is terminated with an error.
  • Ignore: Message processing is ended as defined by your configuration settings.
  • Specified Receiver: The message is sent to a fixed predefined receiver.

How the Migration Tool migrates CBR scenarios from SAP Process Orchestration to SAP Cloud Integration

Migration Wizard:

In the Migration Wizard, after completing Step 1 – Select Process Orchestration System and Step 2 – Select Process Orchestration Artifacts

the next page is Step 3 – Choose Pattern and Approach. In this step, you need to select the appropriate migration pattern and the corresponding approach based on your integration scenario.

Note: Content-based scenarios can be migrated using both the CBR (Content-Based Routing) and RL (Recipient List) patterns. However, it is recommended to choose the CBR pattern when the message is ultimately routed to only a single receiver

  • Content-Based Routing (CBR) Pattern: Multiple receivers can be configured, but at runtime the system evaluates message content and conditions and forwards the message to only one final receiver.
  • Recipient List (RL) Pattern: In the Recipient List pattern, the message can be sent to multiple receivers, with or without conditions. It is used for one-to-many routing where several receivers are eligible to receive the same message.

Note: In SAP PO (Process Orchestration), there is no strict technical distinction between CBR and RL. Both are configured through Receiver Determination rules, and the behavior depends on how the conditions and receivers are defined.

Step 4 and Step 5 are used to import the required mapping objects from your SAP Process Orchestration system.

Step 6 is where you provide the name of the Integration Flow, and

Step 7 allows you to review the objects that will be migrated to SAP Cloud Integration.

After a successful migration, a Summary Page is displayed, providing key insights and direct navigation links to the migrated artifacts in Cloud Integration.

Now let us understand how the Migration Tool generates the Content-Based Routing pattern based on the existing integration configuration and its key flow steps.

Note: This blog primarily focuses on understanding and applying the standard approach.

Standard Approach

After a successful migration, the Integration Flow is typically split into two integration processes - the first acting as the Inbound Integration Process and the second as the Outbound Integration Process.

Note: Both processes are internally connected using the ProcessDirect adapter, enabling seamless message transfer between the inbound and outbound integration Processes.

Inbound Integration Process:

This process receives the message from the sender, sets the sender system and message type, and performs receiver determination where the defined receiver rules and routing conditions are evaluated. The content-based routing logic is executed here to identify the appropriate receiver based on the message content.

Content Modifier – Receiver Conditions & Interface Conditions:

The Content Modifier stores the predefined conditions derived from Receiver Determination and Interface Determination. The local rules, receiver rules, and inbound interface conditions configured in SAP PO are migrated and maintained here, where they are listed and used during runtime evaluation for routing decisions

Let us understand the migrated content from SAP PO that appears in above Content Modifier – Exchange Properties section.

  • SAP-DYNREC-<Receiver Communication Component>:
    SAP-DYNREC is a prefix added to each Receiver Communication Component present in the migrated ICO. 

The value of this property represents the receiver condition migrated from Configured Receivers - Rules. If a condition is defined in SAP PO, it is carried over here; otherwise, the value is set to true(), indicating unconditional routing eligibility for that receiver.

  • SAP-IFDET-<Receiver Communication Component>~IFx:
    SAP-IFDET is the prefix used for Interface Determination and is also added to the Receiver Communication Component name.     The suffix ~IFx acts as an interface index indicator, which increments based on the number of receiver interfaces  maintained for that receiver.

For example, if Receiver_1 has three receiver interfaces, the generated properties would be:

  • SAP-IFDET-Receiver_1~IF1
  • SAP-IFDET-Receiver_1~IF2
  • SAP-IFDET-Receiver_1~IF3

The value of each property reflects the interface condition migrated from the on-premises system. If interface-level conditions exist, they are preserved; if not, the value defaults to true().

These exchange properties collectively store the migrated receiver and interface rules, enabling the integration flow to evaluate routing logic dynamically at runtime.

Receiver Determination:

Receiver Determination is implemented as a Process Call that invokes a local integration process. This local process contains a static receiver-determination Groovy script which evaluates the predefined conditions migrated in the previous step (Receiver Conditions and Interface Conditions). Based on these conditions, the script identifies and returns the appropriate receiver for the message.

Router and No-Receiver Behavior:

The Router is used to forward the message to the specific receiver that was determined based on the evaluated conditions.

In addition to routing, it also manages the No-Receiver Behavior. If no receiver is found, the Migration Tool dynamically generates the configured fallback option. 

For example, it can automatically route the message to a default receiver (in this example, RL_Receiver_4).

Alternatively, if the configuration is set to Error = an Exception Event is generated, and if set to Ignore = an End Event step is created to terminate the flow without further processing.

Outbound Integration Process:

The Outbound Integration Process contains the mappings, receiver communication channels, and the configured receivers, and is responsible for delivering the processed message to the final target system.

Router:

In the Outbound Integration Process, the Router uses the receiver determined in the Inbound Integration Process to bind the corresponding receiver interface/mappings to the specific receiver via its associated communication channel. The message is then forwarded along the appropriate route based on the evaluated conditions, ensuring it reaches the correct receiver.

Pipeline Approach

In this section, we will migrate the same example using the Pipeline Approach.

Key Highlights in the Migration Wizard:

After selecting the Pipeline Approach for your migration use case, the Migration Tool allows you to modify alternative partner information, as well as review and update the migrated receiver/interface conditions and endpoints, as illustrated in the screenshot below.

After a successful migration, you can view the generated artifacts and navigate to them directly.

In the Pipeline Approach, scenario-specific Inbound and Outbound Integration Flows are created. The Outbound Integration Flow is generated for each receiver configured in the respective ICO, ensuring that messages are routed correctly based on the original configuration.

The Partner Directory tab provides an overview of the created Partner ID along with their associated parameters.

On the Binary Parameters tab of the Partner Directory, you can see that a binary parameter with the ID receiverDetermination has been created. This parameter contains the XSLT used to determine the appropriate receivers during message processing.

For more information, See Using the Partner Directory in the Pipeline Concept

For a deeper understanding of the Pipeline Approach, please refer to the following references:

Migration Tooling now supporting migration via the pipeline approach

Applying the pipeline concept for an integration scenario in general

Learn here the differences and characteristics of each approach to choose the one that aligns best with your migration needs Standard vs Pipeline Approach

Post-Migration Activities – Key Points to Keep in Mind:

 After successfully migrating CBR Integration Flow, the following activities should be verified and completed:

  1. Verify Migrated Artifacts: Ensure that mappings (local and global), Function Libraries, imported archives, and Local UDFs are migrated correctly and functioning as expected.
  2. Adapter Configuration: Adapter parameters are externalized. Verify the configurations and update them as needed to match the target environment.
  3. Adapter Conversions: Adapter conversions such as JSON-to-XML or CSV-to-XML are migrated automatically. Check the configuration in the conversion flow steps and adjust if necessary.
  4. Security Artifacts: The Migration Tool does not create or import security artifacts in Cloud Integration. Manual creation of security materials (certificates, keystores, etc.) is a prerequisite before deploying the Integration Flow.
  5. Reusable Mapping Options: If the “Enable Reusable Mapping” option was selected during migration, ensure that the standalone artifacts are deployed first before deploying the main Integration Flow.

For other manual activities for specific senarios, please refer to the section “Manual Activities Depending on Integration Scenario” in the P2P Blog from series

Conclusion:

Content-Based Routing (CBR) scenarios can be successfully migrated to Cloud Integration using the Migration Tool, preserving receiver determination, routing conditions, mappings, and adapter configurations. While most artifacts are migrated automatically, post-migration verification and manual activities - such as security setup and reusable mapping deployment - are essential. Following the recommended steps ensures that your integration flows continue to run reliably and efficiently in the Cloud Integration environment.

What Will Be Covered in the Next Parts of Master Blog Series:

In the next parts of this blog series, we will explore the following topics in depth with a detailed and comprehensive walkthrough:

  • Extended Receiver Determination using the Pipeline Approach
  • Operation-Specific Receiver Determination and Multi-Operation Scenarios
  • Content-Based Routing (CBR) – Synchronous Use Cases

entry.title: Fiori changed value to appear when deleting table entry
entry.content:
In S4HANA Public Cloud, I have a custom app using RAP that has Gross Amount field.

I  wanted to make the Gross Amount value updated whenever I change the Unit Price in the Conditions Table.

Gross Amount is part of one entity while the Conditions Table is another.

When I add a new entry and enter a Unit Price value, the Gross Amount value updates. But when I delete an entry having a Unit Price, the Gross Amount doesnt update.

I've already applied side effects and determinations in my behavior definition to trigger calculation during delete and reflect changes in the Gross Amount. I checked the draft table and the Gross Amount is updated but the value doesnt change in the UI. 

determination CalcGrossAmtByDelete on modify { delete; }  
side effects
  {
    field UnitPrice
    affects
        field _Entity1.GrossAmount;

    $self
    affects
        field _Entity1.GrossAmount;
  }

entry.title: Re: SAP PM Task List Selection Based on Maintenance Activity Type
Re: SAP PM Task List Selection Based on Maintenance Activ...
entry.content: To clarify you are not talking about Maintenance Plan (strategy) driven Work Orders but Work Orders created from Notifications? If that's the case then you will need some sort of enhancement to drive the Task List search based on the 6  Maintenance Activity Type. If the people are selecting the Task Lists manually when they are creating the Work Order then the Task List 'Usage' field would be ideal to line up with the 6 Maintenance Activity Types. This would simplify their search for the correct Task List then you can do the enhancement to automate it later.

entry.title: Re: Default Public Layout in Fiori App

Ah yes Fiori views and layouts. Saving these publicly is not the same as the GUI transactions. When you create these Public variants they do appear in all user's list but they have to select the favourite star so they can see it. Also, you setting as default is for you and you only.

You will have to get each user to open up their variant list then select 'Manage'. All they need to do is to select the star beside the Public variant then it will be visible for them to select. They have to 'Set as Default' for that to work.

Women Talk SAP é um evento que será conduzido  pelas mulheres da comunidade SAP;

Com o objetivo de promover networking e Empoderamento;

Promoverá  conversas que humanizam a tecnologia, onde a troca de experiências reais e o acolhimento fortalecem o protagonismo diversidade no ecossistema SAP.

entry.title: Re: Using Migration Assessment to Plan Your SAP Process Orchestration to SAP Integration Suite Migra
entry.content: @Shashi  Big migration projects are very sensitive to time and Normal migration strategy doesnt work with them. For example for migrating 1000 integrations on SAP PO I would need atleast 2600 - 3000 days for a single resource (less for multiple resources) , no customer would agree to this. I think this is where pipeline helps. Pipeline concept will make your migrations faster but it goes against the principle that migration is an opportunity to soft upgrade like  simplify, make the integrations more robust, redesign some with API's etc. So to answer your question I think use pipeline concept for large customers IF they are not happy with the effort estimates you provide and call these estimates too large. SAP's migration tool is getting better day by day and that's what I have used for years as whatever tool you use you need to spend "SAME" effort in cleaning the integration up or for the finishing touch. BUT for large customers do use INT4's regression testing tool it can bring down testing effort by 90%

entry.title: Re: Need to add PO to Invoice, but didn't find the place...
entry.content:
Hello customer,

If it's not possible for you to add the missing PO number to the invoice in the Finance & Legal dashboard in SAP for Me, then very possible the corresponding contract is currently not eligible for PO submission via SAP for Me.

For additional support in submitting the PO number, please use the 'Get assistance' option available under the 'Manage Contract' menu on the last column of the Contracts table.

The 'Get assistance' button is also available under the Billing tab. Select the invoice check-box first, then the button is clickable. 

The submitted form will reach the PO Collection team directly and they will be able to assist further.

This is documented in KBA 3707175 - Manage PO Numbers button is not clickable - SAP for Me, kindly refer to the KBA for more details.

Hope this helps.

Thanks and regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Year-End or Mid-Year Valuation Not Permitted for Vendor or Customer in Foreign Currency Valuation
entry.content:
When executing Foreign Currency Valuation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the system raises an error stating that Year-End or Mid-Year Valuation is not permitted for the selected valuation area when Vendor or Customer open items are included in the run.

The Year-End and Mid-Year indicators are visible in the job parameters. However, activating them together with Vendor or Customer valuation results in an error. The business requirement is to perform year-end foreign currency valuation for open items without an automatic reversal in the following fiscal period.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Year-End or Mid-Year Valuation Not Permitted for Vend...
entry.content:
This is standard system behavior.

Foreign Currency Valuation differentiates between open item valuation and balance valuation. Vendor and Customer accounts are open item managed accounts. For these accounts, valuation postings represent unrealized exchange rate differences. These postings are automatically reset by an inverse posting in the subsequent fiscal period. This ensures that the full realized exchange rate difference is correctly determined at clearing.

The Year-End and Mid-Year Valuation indicators require delta logic to be active for the valuation area. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, delta logic is not generally available and is activated by SAP only for specific countries based on legal requirements. If delta logic is not active for the valuation area, the system prevents Vendor or Customer valuation when these indicators are selected.

To perform year-end valuation for Vendor or Customer open items, execute the standard Foreign Currency Valuation run as of the required key date without selecting the Year-End or Mid-Year indicator. The resulting postings will impact the financial statements of the selected fiscal year. The automatic reversal in the subsequent fiscal period does not affect the closed fiscal year reporting.

If non-reversing postings are required for specific business reasons, this must be handled outside of standard Foreign Currency Valuation, for example through manual journal entries.

entry.title: Unlock the Power of People Management with Joule
entry.content:
In the modern workplace, managers face growing challenges in fostering team development, improving performance, and identifying future leaders. SAP’s Joule introduces the People Manager Assistant—a role-specific AI tool designed to empower managers to efficiently support their teams.

The People Manager Assistant goes beyond typical management tools. It uses intelligent analysis of employee data, feedback, and performance reviews to pinpoint areas for improvement. By providing proactive insights on development goals, personalized growth plans, and succession planning timelines, this assistant helps managers streamline the talent development process. Imagine being able to foster employee growth with precision while saving valuable time to focus on strategic leadership.

Whether creating clear development roadmaps or analyzing workforce performance, the People Manager Assistant turns complex people-related decisions into actionable intelligence. It's not just about managing teams—it's about empowering them. With Joule managers can inspire growth, prioritize team alignment, and drive performance like never before.

Let’s look at an example of an AI Assistant in action:

Disclaimer: The names used in the following story are fictional, and any resemblance to actual persons is purely coincidental.

Christine’s potential needed direction, so her manager, Alex, tapped the People Manager Assistant for insights. The assistant identified key growth areas and crafted actionable goals. Later, Tara was assessed for succession, revealing promise with development needs. In minutes, structured plans were created, ensuring growth and leadership continuity for the organization.

 

Summary:

• Joule empowers faster decision-making and streamlines workflows using harmonized data and trusted architecture.
• Role-specific AI assistants offer tailored support to handle complex tasks efficiently in various business functions.
• The People Manager Assistant analyzes employee data to develop growth plans, identify successors, and enhance team performance.
• Joule Agents deliver measurable improvements, optimizing productivity across areas like HR Operations and Accounts Receivable Management.

entry.title: Benefits of Maintenance Planning Overview Fiori Application
entry.content:
In the fast-paced world of industrial asset management, the difference between a high-performing plant and a costly bottleneck often comes down to how quickly a maintenance planner can identify and react to issues.

In the legacy SAP environment, this meant digging through multiple transaction codes (T-codes) to find overdue orders or missing parts. With SAP S/4HANA, the Maintenance Planning Overview (App ID: F2828) Fiori application changes the game by consolidating critical data into a single, proactive dashboard.

Here is an overview of why this app is becoming the "command center" for modern maintenance planners.

What is the Maintenance Planning Overview App?

The Maintenance Planning Overview is an analytical Fiori application designed as a "one-stop shop" for Maintenance Planners. Built on the SAP Fiori Overview Page (OVP) floorplan, it uses interactive "cards" to display high-level KPIs and real-time data visualizations.

Instead of searching for problems, the problems are pushed to you. The app analyzes factors like open notifications, missing spare parts, and overdue orders based on a reference period (e.g., "This Week" or "Next Week") that you define.

Key Features: Your Planning Toolkit

The app is organized into task-oriented cards. Each card serves a specific purpose in the maintenance lifecycle:

1. Planning & Backlog Management

  • Orders for Planning: Identifies maintenance orders that have been created but not yet released. It visualizes these by priority or order type using pie charts.

  • Outstanding Notifications: Shows notifications that haven't been assigned to an order yet, allowing you to catch work requests before they fall through the cracks.

2. Material & Procurement Visibility

  • Purchase Requisitions/Orders Not Approved: Tracks non-stock materials required for maintenance. You can see which parts might cause delays because the requisition hasn't been approved or the purchase order hasn't been sent.

  • Non-Stock Materials Not Available: A critical card that flags ordered parts that will not arrive by the required start date of the maintenance order.

3. Execution Tracking

  • Overdue Orders: Highlights released orders where the end date has passed but the operations haven't been finally confirmed.

  • Orders Not Technically Completed: Provides a list of orders that are physically finished but haven't been closed out in the system, which is vital for accurate financial reporting.

 

 

The app typically includes the following standard Fiori cards:

1. Maintenance Order Cards

  • Orders for Planning: Visualizes maintenance orders that have been created but not yet released. It usually breaks them down by Priority or Order Type.

  • Overdue Orders: Analyzes released maintenance orders where the basic end date has already passed, but the operations have not been finally confirmed.

  • Orders for Completion: Specifically highlights orders where all operations are confirmed (CNF) but the order header has not yet been set to Technically Completed (TECO).

  • Jobs (Order Operations): Displays a list of upcoming or current maintenance "jobs" (operations) that require attention or execution within the defined reference period.

2. Maintenance Notification Cards

  • Outstanding Notifications: Displays notifications that have been created but are not yet assigned to a maintenance order. This helps prevent a backlog of unaddressed work requests.

  • Recently Created Notifications: Gives a quick look at the newest requests entering the system, categorized by their processing status.

3. Procurement & Material Cards

  • Non-Stock Materials Not Available: This is a high-value card that flags non-stock components (parts ordered specifically for an order) that are not expected to arrive by the order's start date.

  • Purchase Requisitions Not Approved: Identifies PRs linked to maintenance orders that are stalled in the approval workflow, which could delay planned repairs.

  • Purchase Requisitions Not Converted: Shows approved requisitions that have not yet been turned into formal Purchase Orders (POs).

4. Technical Object Cards

  • Technical Object Damages: Often used to visualize which assets are experiencing the most frequent issues, categorized by damage codes or object parts.

     

Key Features of These Cards

  • Filter Sensitivity: All cards react to the global filters at the top of the app (e.g., Planning Plant, Planner Group, or Time Period).

  • Drill-Down Capability: Clicking the header of a card takes you to a detailed list view (like the "Manage Maintenance Orders" app), while clicking a specific data point (like a slice of a pie chart) filters that list further.

  • Personalization: Users can hide cards they don't need or rearrange them on the dashboard to put the most critical KPIs at the top

entry.title: HANA Client Version Impact on HANABulkCopy Performance
entry.content:
I am using SAP Business One 10.0 for SAP HANA FP 2505 with the following environment:

  • SAP HANA 2.0 Enterprise Edition SPS 05 Revision 2.00.059.09.1686579463 (fa/hana2sp05)
  • SAP HANA Client version 2.17.22
  • SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 15 SP5

I am encountering an unexpected performance issue when using HANABulkCopy from the SAP HANA ADO.NET library to copy data from a .NET DataTable into an SAP HANA column table.

Issue

With SAP HANA Client 2.17.22, running HANABulkCopy takes 30–40 minutes, even when inserting only 2 rows, and sometimes the operation times out.

What I tried

I installed a newer SAP HANA Client version 2.27.19, and with the exact same code and table:

  • Inserting 2 rows or even 20,000 rows now takes only 1–2 seconds.
  • No timeouts occur.
  • Performance is consistently stable.

My question

The SAP Business One Administrator’s Guide for SAP HANA recommends using SAP HANA Client version 2.17.22 for this B1 version.

So I am trying to understand:

  • Why does version 2.17.22 cause such extreme delays with HANABulkCopy?
  • Why does upgrading to 2.27.19 immediately resolve the issue, even though it is not the version officially recommended for SAP Business One?

Has anyone else experienced this behavior with HANABulkCopy on older HANA Client versions?
Is there any known limitation, bug, or compatibility issue with 2.17.22 that would explain this performance drop?

Any guidance or insights would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Publish events from CAP Node.js applications to Event Mesh of Integration Suite
entry.content:
In this blog I’ll show how to publish events from a CAP application to SAP Integration Suite’s Event Mesh. Both the CAP application and Event Mesh are located in the same BTP subaccount. I’ll skip the setup steps for Event Mesh and focus specifically on publishing events from the CAP application.

Step 1: start by creating a new CAP Node.js application in Business Application Studio:

cds init emis-samebtp
touch srv/service.cds
touch srv/service.js

Step 2: in service.cds, define a simple service with a function that triggers an event:

service GreetingService {
  function helloEvent(to: String) returns String;
}

In service.js, implement the function to emit an event to Event Mesh:

const eventTopic = 'cap/demo/v1/sample/hello-event';

module.exports = async (srv) => {
  const messaging = await cds.connect.to('messaging');

  srv.on('helloEvent', async (req) => {
    const target = req.data.to;
    const data = { To: target, Message: 'Hello world events!' };
    
    await messaging.emit(eventTopic, data);
    req.notify(`Event sent > ${eventTopic}`);
    
    return `Hello event: ${target}`;
  });
};

Step 3: add the necessary services for authentication and messaging:

cds add xsuaa --for-production
cds add enterprise-messaging-shared --for-production

Then update your package.json to configure the messaging service:

"cds": {
  "server": { "index": true },
  "requires": {
    "[production]": {
      "auth": "xsuaa",
      "messaging": {
        "kind": "enterprise-messaging-shared",
        "publishPrefix": "stc/emis/",
        "format": "cloudevents",
        "vcap": {
          "label": "event-mesh-message-client",
          "plan": "message-client",
          "name": "emis-samebtp-messaging"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Step 4: generate the MTA deployment descriptor:

npm install
cds build --production
cds add mta

Edit the generated mta.yaml to ensure the resource definitions match your package.json configuration.

Step 5: run the build and deploy commands to push your application to BTP. 

cds build
cf deploy mta_archives/your-archive.mtar

Step 6: Testing the Integration

Navigate to the Event Mesh section in SAP Integration Suite. Select a queue and subscribe to the topic "stc/emis/cap/demo/v1/sample/hello-event". 

Then use the OAuth client credentials from XSUAA to call your CAP service "https://<your-hostname>:443/odata/v4/greeting/helloEvent?to=Mark".

 If everything is configured correctly, you’ll receive a response and see the event appear in your Event Mesh queue.

This setup is ideal for scenarios where you need real-time eventing between BTP applications and other SAP or non-SAP systems. Give it a try and let me know how it goes in the comments!
